title: Modular shelving system
abstract: The present disclosure concerns a modular shelving system comprising a plurality of shelves (34) each configured with opposite beam bearing edges (40), at least two front columns and two rear columns, each column configured of column segments, and pairs of shelf support beams (50) corresponding with the number of shelves (34), where the length of said column segments and said shelf support beams (50) does not exceeding the length of the shelves (34) wherein, at an assembled position, the at least two front columns and two rear columns are disposed at an upright position with the pairs of shelf support beams (50) parallelly articulated between opposite rear columns and opposite front columns, each shelf support beam (50) bearing below a respective beam-bearing edge of a shelf (34) and supporting the shelf (34); and wherein at a storage position the plurality of shelves (34) are stackably nested, and the column segments are disposed over the stack of shelves (34) in parallel with the beam-bearing edges, with at least some of the shelf support beams (50) received within a longitudinal hollow of the column segments.
claims: Claims CLAIMS: 1. A modular shelving system comprising a plurality of shelves each configured with opposite beam bearing edges, at least two front columns and two rear columns, each column configured of column segments, and pairs of shelf support beams corresponding with the number of shelves, where the length of said column segments and said shelf support beams does not exceeding the length of the shelves; wherein, at an assembled position, the at least two front columns and two rear columns are disposed at an upright position with the pairs of shelf support beams parallelly articulated between opposite rear columns and opposite front columns, each shelf support beam bearing below a respective beam-bearing edge of a shelf and supporting the shelf; and wherein at a storage position the plurality of shelves are stackably nested, and the column segments are disposed over the stack of shelves in parallel with the beam bearing edges, with at least some of the shelf support beams received within a longitudinal hollow of the column segments. 2. The modular shelving system of claim 1, comprising four columns, each comprising two segments; a plurality of stackable nestable shelves, each shelf configured with a beam-bearing edge along a front edge and a rear edge thereof; pairs of shelf support beams corresponding with the number of shelves; coupling members for coupling ends of the shelf support beams between the columns; and two or more crossbar braces for engaging between two respective side columns, wherein the length of the shelves is substantially similar to, or slightly greater than the length of the column segments and the length of the shelf support beams, and wherein at a storage position all column segments are placed over stacked shelves, each column segment coaxially receiving a shelf support beam. 3. The modular shelving system of claim 1, wherein where the number of shelf support beams exceeds the number of column segments, at the storage position, a shelf support beam is positioned under a respective beam-bearing edge of a bottom-most shelf of the stack of shelves. 4. The modular shelving system of claim 1, wherein a shelf of said plurality of shelves is mounted over two shelf support beams and is detachably attached thereto. 5. The modular shelving system of claim 4, wherein attachment of a shelf to a shelf support beam is facilitated through snap engagement or friction arresting of the longitudinal grove over the shelf support beam. 6. The modular shelving system of claim 1, wherein said shelf support beams are fixedly, though detachably, secured between front and rear support columns, respectively. 7. The modular shelving system of claim 1, wherein the shelves are of substantially thin nominal wall thickness, whereby reinforcement thereof is facilitated by reinforcing ribs configured at least at a bottom face of a shelf, with several transversally extending supplementary reinforcing elements extending between the longitudinally extending beam-bearing edges, giving rise to a ladder-like structure. 8. The modular shelving system of claim 7, wherein the maximal depth of the supplemental reinforcing is similar to, or greater than, the maximal height of the shelf support beams. 9. The modular shelving system of claim 7, wherein the supplemental reinforcing elements are depressions extending from a bottom face of a shelf, between opposite beam-bearing edges of the shelf. 10. The modular shelving system of claim 1, wherein, at the storage position, the height of a knocked-down modular shelving system corresponds to a height of the stacked shelves and the thickness of a column segment. 11. The modular shelving system of claim 7, wherein the supplementary reinforcing elements are depressions configured into a bottom face of the shelf, extending between the beam bearing edges. 12. The modular shelving system of claim 1, further comprising a plurality of auxiliary elements, wherein at the storage position said auxiliary elements are disposed at a space extending between neighboring supplementary reinforcing elements of a bottom-most shelf of the stack of shelves. 13. The modular shelving system of claim 1, wherein the beam- bearing edge is a narrow longitudinal grove extending along the respective front and rear edge of a shelf, and configured for bearing over a shelf support beam. 14. The modular shelving system of claim 1, wherein the longitudinal groove at least partially embraces a top portion of the respective shelf support beam. 15. The modular shelving system of claim 1, wherein a shelf support beam is connected between two facing surfaces of left and right columns. 16. The modular shelving system of claim 1, wherein connection of a shelf support beam to a column is facilitated directly or by a coupler member attachable at a respective end of a shelf support beam and configured for detachable attachment to a column. 17. The modular shelving system of claim 16, wherein a coupler member comprises a beam receiving portion snugly mounted over an end of the shelf support beam. 18. The modular shelving system of claim 16, wherein a coupler member is detachably attachable to a support column by a sliding joint. 19. The modular shelving system of claim 1, wherein the column segments are configured with a plurality of recesses, at least along one face thereof, said recesses configured for slidingly receiving a projecting lug extending from an end of a shelf support beam or from a coupler member attachable to an end of a shelf support beam. 20. The modular shelving system of claim 7, wherein at an assembled position each shelf with the associated shelf support beams gives rise to ladder-like truss, comprising longitudinally extending from the beam-bearing edge and the supplementary reinforcing elements configured at the bottom face of the shelf and extending from opposite shelf support beams and traversing at a right angle. 21. The modular shelving system of claim 1, wherein crossbar braces are articulated between respective left side columns and right side columns, for reinforcing and stabilizing the assembled shelving system. 22. The modular shelving system of claim 21, wherein a crossbar brace is articulated to a column at a coupling site of coextensively articulated column segments, thereby engaging both column segments. 23. The modular shelving system of claim 1, wherein the total height (H Assy ) of the modular shelving system at the storage position corresponds to the total shelves nesting height (Hnest) plus the thickness of a column segment (Tc): HAssy= H nest +Tc, wherein the total shelves nesting height (H nest ) of a stack of stacked shelves comprising a number (n) of shelves corresponds with the total height of one shelf (H S heif ) and the height of reinforcing ribs (H r ib) at a bottom face of the shelf multiplied by the number of shelfs leSS 1 : Hnest = Hshelf+Hrib *(n-l). 24. The modular shelving system of claim 1, wherein the footprint of the modular shelving system at the storage position is substantially equal to the footprint of a single shelf of the modular shelving system. 25. A shelf for use in conjunction with a shelving system, the shelf being a planar member made of molded plastic material and having substantially thin nominal wall thickness, said shelf being reinforced by a plurality of reinforcing ribs configured at least at a bottom face of the shelf, with several transversally extending supplementary reinforcing elements extending between longitudinally extending beam-bearing edges disposed at front and rear edges of the shelf, giving rise to a ladder-like structure. 26. The shelf of 25, wherein each shelf is reinforced by a plurality of warp and weft reinforcing ribs extending form a bottom face of the shelf. 27. The shelf of claim 25, wherein at least portions between warp and weft ribs is hollowed out, thereby reducing overall weight of the shelf.
